title: pilosella bauhini subsp. polyanthema
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. polyanthema is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Pilosella bauhini plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. polyanthema include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
